key_files.relevance: Contains the window configuration that needs updating (width: 1024, height: 768, resizable: true)
Lines 363-366 contain the Window Configuration spec that currently says 800x700, non-resizable, centered
CSS layout uses 100vw/100vh so it fills the window. Has responsive breakpoint at 820px for metadata panel stacking. Metadata panel is 350px wide - works fine at 1024px but would be too wide at 800px.
Design tokens - no hardcoded pixel window dimensions
HTML structure - no hardcoded window dimensions. Uses viewport units via CSS.

patterns.name: CSS Viewport Fill
patterns.description: CSS uses 100vw/100vh for the #app element, so the layout adapts to whatever window size Tauri configures.

patterns.name: Responsive Breakpoint
patterns.description: CSS has a media query at max-width: 820px that stacks the metadata panel below the image on narrow screens.

patterns.name: Metadata Panel Width
patterns.description: The metadata panel has a fixed width of 350px when displayed side-by-side. With an 800px window this is problematic (only ~450px for image), but at 1024px it is comfortable.

architecture.window_configuration: Defined entirely in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json under app.windows[0]. CSS/HTML adapts to window size via viewport units.
architecture.spec_documentation: specs/spec.md lines 363-366 describe the Window Configuration section.
architecture.no_runtime_resize: No JavaScript code sets or modifies window dimensions at runtime. Tauri configuration is the sole source of truth.
architecture.decision_rationale: Option B (keep 1024x768) is correct because: (1) metadata panel at 350px requires at least 800px total, and (2) CSS responsive breakpoint at 820px would trigger at 800px width, forcing a degraded vertical layout. 1024x768 provides comfortable layout. Option A (non-resizable) is correct per spec recommendation for predictable layout.

testing.unit_tests: No unit tests for window dimensions - this is a configuration-only change
testing.verification: Can verify tauri.conf.json values with jq/JSON parsing. Visual verification requires running the app.
testing.css_audit: CSS uses viewport-relative units (100vw, 100vh), so it works at any window size. The 820px breakpoint means 1024px keeps the side-by-side layout.

conventions: Changes to config must be reflected in spec and vice versa
